name: Persian Kuku Sabzi
steps: Whisk the eggs with the herbs and flour.
Pour into a hot, oiled skillet and cook, covered, over low heat 8-10 minutes until set.
Flip and cook 2-3 more minutes.
steps_detailed: Whisk the eggs with the herbs, flour, and onion until well combined.
Pour into a hot, oiled skillet and cook, covered, over low heat 10-12 minutes until mostly set.
Flip carefully and cook 3-4 more minutes until fully cooked through.
Kuku sabzi, a dense herb frittata, is a beloved Persian breakfast and Nowruz (Persian New Year) tradition.

name: Persian Naan-e Barbari
steps: Mix the flour, baking powder, a pinch of salt, and warm water into a soft dough. Rest 20 minutes.
Shape into a flat oval and bake at 450F for 12-15 minutes until golden.
steps_detailed: Mix the flour, baking powder, a pinch of salt, oil, and warm water into a soft, elastic dough. Knead well and rest 25-30 minutes.
Shape into a long, flat oval, dimple the surface with your fingers, and scatter with sesame seeds.
Bake at 450F (230C) for 12-16 minutes until golden and crisp - barbari, a thick, ridged flatbread, is a staple Persian breakfast bread.

name: Persian Feta and Herbs
steps: Arrange the feta, herbs, and walnuts on a plate.
Serve with warm flatbread for wrapping.
steps_detailed: Arrange the feta, fresh herbs, walnuts, and dates on a plate.
Serve with warm flatbread for wrapping bites together - a simple, elegant Persian breakfast spread, sabzi khordan.

name: Persian Ash Reshteh
steps: Saute the onion until deeply golden.
Simmer the noodles and beans with the onion and 2 cups water for 15-18 minutes.
Stir in the cilantro and serve.
steps_detailed: Saute the onion and garlic until deeply golden and caramelized, 12-15 minutes - this forms the aromatic base.
Simmer the noodles and beans with the onion mixture in 2.25 cups water for 18-22 minutes until the noodles are tender.
Stir in the cilantro and finish with the lime juice - ash reshteh, a hearty herb and noodle soup, is a beloved Persian dish, especially for Nowruz.

name: Persian Baghali Polo
steps: Cook the rice with the beans and dill.
Toss with the oil before serving.
steps_detailed: Cook the rice according to package directions, folding in the beans and dill during the last few minutes of cooking.
Toss with the oil and butter before serving - baghali polo, dill rice with fava beans, is a treasured Persian side, traditionally paired with lamb.

name: Persian Tahchin

ingredients.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps: Mix the cooked rice with the yogurt and egg.
Fold in the shredded chicken and press into a small baking dish.
Bake at 375F for 30-35 minutes until golden and set.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps_detailed: Mix the cooked rice with the yogurt, egg, saffron, and melted butter until well combined.
Fold in the shredded chicken, reserving some to layer in the middle, and press firmly into a small buttered baking dish.
Bake at 375F (190C) for 32-40 minutes until the top is golden and the bottom has formed a crisp, golden crust.
Tahchin, a saffron rice cake with a prized crispy crust, is a beloved and impressive Persian dish.

name: Persian Zereshk Polo

ingredients.ingredient: Cranberries, dried, sweetened (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps: Dissolve the saffron in a splash of warm water.
Toss the cooked rice with the butter, saffron water, and dried cranberries.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Cranberries, dried, sweetened (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Dissolve the saffron in a tablespoon of warm water.
Briefly warm the dried cranberries (standing in for tart barberries) in the butter with the sugar, 2-3 minutes.
Toss the cooked rice with the saffron water and the buttered cranberries - zereshk polo, jeweled with tart berries, is a treasured Persian dish, traditionally paired with chicken.

name: Persian Ghormeh Sabzi

ingredients.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps: Brown the beef.
Add the beans, herbs, lime juice, and a splash of water, and simmer 1-1.25 hours until tender.
Serve with rice.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps_detailed: Brown the beef with the onion, 8-10 minutes.
Add the beans, cilantro, fenugreek, lime juice (standing in for dried lime, traditionally used), and 3/4 cup water.
Simmer, covered, 1.25-1.5 hours until the beef is very tender and the sauce has deepened into a dark green.
Serve with rice - ghormeh sabzi, a beloved herb stew, is widely considered Iran's national dish.

name: Persian Chelo Kabab
steps: Mix the beef with the grated onion, and shape into flattened logs.
Grill or pan-fry until well charred and cooked through.
Serve over the rice, finished with a pinch of saffron dissolved in warm water.
steps_detailed: Mix the beef thoroughly with the grated onion until smooth and slightly sticky, then shape into flattened logs around skewers if you have them.
Grill or pan-fry, 4-6 minutes per side, until well charred and cooked through.
Dissolve the saffron in a tablespoon of warm water and drizzle over the cooked rice along with the butter.
Serve the kabab over the saffron rice with the tomatoes - chelo kabab, grilled meat over saffron rice, is Iran's most iconic dish.

name: Persian Fesenjan
steps: Brown the chicken with the onion.
Blend the ground walnuts with the pomegranate juice and a splash of water into a sauce.
Add to the chicken and simmer 25-30 minutes until thickened.
steps_detailed: Brown the chicken with the onion and turmeric, 8-10 minutes.
Blend the ground walnuts with the pomegranate juice, sugar, and a splash of water into a thick sauce.
Add the sauce to the chicken and simmer 28-35 minutes, stirring occasionally, until deeply thickened and rich.
Fesenjan, walnut and pomegranate stew, is a treasured Persian dish for special occasions, prized for its sweet-tart depth.

name: Persian Abgoosht (Dizi)

ingredients.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps: Simmer the beef with the beans, potato, and tomatoes for 1.25-1.5 hours until everything is very tender.
Mash roughly before serving with bread.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps_detailed: Simmer the beef with the onion, turmeric, beans, potato, and tomatoes in 2.5 cups water for 1.5-1.75 hours until everything is completely tender.

Traditionally the broth is served first as a soup, then the solids are mashed together and eaten separately with bread - abgoosht (also called dizi, after the pot it's cooked in) is one of Iran's most cherished, ancient comfort dishes.

name: Persian Hummus with Walnuts

ingredients.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids
Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps: Blend the chickpeas, tahini, and lime juice with a splash of water until smooth.
Top with the chopped walnuts and serve with flatbread.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids
Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps_detailed: Blend the chickpeas, tahini, lime juice, and garlic with a splash of water until very smooth.
Top with the chopped walnuts and serve with warm flatbread.

name: Persian Roasted Nuts (Ajil)

ingredients.ingredient: Peanuts, all types, dry-roasted, with salt
Raisins, dark, seedless (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps: Combine the nuts and raisins.
Serve as a simple snack mix.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Peanuts, all types, dry-roasted, with salt
Raisins, dark, seedless (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Combine the nuts, raisins, and dates.
Ajil, a mixed nut and dried fruit snack, is a common Persian teatime staple.

name: Persian Mast-o Khiar

ingredients.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps: Mix the yogurt with the cucumber and dill.
Season with salt and chill before serving.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Plain Organic Greek Yogurt, Plain

steps_detailed: Mix the yogurt with the cucumber, dill, walnuts, and garlic.
Season with salt and chill at least 15 minutes before serving - mast-o khiar, a cooling yogurt-cucumber dip, is a Persian meal staple.

name: Persian Nan-e Shirini
steps: Cream the butter and sugar, then mix in the flour to form a soft dough.
Shape into small rounds, brush with egg wash, and bake at 350F for 14-18 minutes until golden.
steps_detailed: Cream the butter and sugar together until light, then mix in the flour and cardamom until a soft dough forms.
Shape into small rounds, brush with the egg wash, and bake at 350F (175C) for 15-20 minutes until golden.
Nan-e shirini, simple sweet cookies, are a common Persian teatime treat.

name: Persian Sholeh Zard
steps: Simmer the rice in 1.5 cups water with the sugar, saffron, and cinnamon for 25-30 minutes until thick and golden.
Chill or serve warm.
steps_detailed: Simmer the rice in 1.75 cups water with the sugar, saffron, and cinnamon over medium-low heat, stirring occasionally, 28-35 minutes until thick and richly golden.
Top with chopped pistachios and serve warm or chilled - sholeh zard, saffron rice pudding, is a treasured Persian dessert, often made as a votive offering.

name: Persian Halva

ingredients.ingredient: Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps: Warm the sugar with a splash of water until it forms a syrup.
Stir the syrup into the tahini with the rosewater until it thickens.
Press into a small dish and chill before slicing.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps_detailed: Warm the sugar with a splash of water over medium heat until it dissolves into a thick syrup, 3-4 minutes.
Remove from heat and stir in the tahini and rosewater vigorously until it thickens and turns pale.
Fold in the chopped pistachios, press into a small lined dish, and chill at least 1 hour until firm.
Persian halva, dense and fudge-like, is traditionally prepared for mourning gatherings as well as everyday sweets.

name: Persian Faloodeh
steps: Cook the noodles until tender, then rinse under cold water.
Warm the sugar with a splash of water and the rosewater into a light syrup, then chill.
Toss the cold noodles with the syrup and lime juice, and freeze 1-2 hours before serving icy cold.
steps_detailed: Cook the noodles (standing in for rice vermicelli, traditionally used) until tender, then rinse thoroughly under cold water and drain well.
Warm the sugar with a splash of water and the rosewater over low heat, 3-4 minutes, into a light syrup, then chill completely.
Toss the cold noodles with the chilled syrup and lime juice, and freeze for 1-2 hours, stirring once partway through, until icy and semi-frozen.
Serve immediately, icy cold - faloodeh, a unique frozen noodle sorbet, is one of the world's oldest known frozen desserts, with roots in ancient Persia.

name: Persian Pomegranate Walnut Sauce
steps: Simmer the pomegranate juice with the ground walnuts and sugar for 8-10 minutes until thickened.
Serve over grilled chicken or vegetables.
steps_detailed: Simmer the pomegranate juice with the ground walnuts, sugar, and cinnamon over medium-low heat, stirring, 10-12 minutes until thickened.
This nods to the same sweet-tart walnut-pomegranate flavor base as fesenjan, in condiment form.

name: Persian Torshi-Style Pickled Vegetables
steps: Combine the carrot, cabbage, and vinegar.
Let sit at least 20 minutes before serving.
steps_detailed: Combine the carrot, cabbage, vinegar, garlic, and cinnamon.
Let sit at least 25-30 minutes so the vegetables lightly pickle - torshi, tangy pickled vegetables, are a staple Persian table condiment.
